Reviewing the Dockerfile and how the runner image is built and configured so we can propose a maintainable design.

[REDACTED]

Explored the repo: the image is built remotely via heredocs in `buildAgenticRunnerImage`, and the image tag is only `gh-sr/agentic-runner:<actions-runner-version>`. Per-runner extra packages would require a tag or identity change so different runners do not share the wrong image. Asking one design choice before finalizing the plan.

[REDACTED]

questions.prompt: Should extra apt packages be configured per runner (each runner block can differ → multiple image variants on the host) or once globally (single extra list shared by every runner_mode: container on that machine)?

options.label: Per runner in each runners.yml entry (flexible; image tag must encode package set)
Global in config (e.g. top-level or under github:) — one shared image for all container runners

content.text:
Incorporating global extra packages: we'll thread config from `ops` into the image build and derive the image tag when extras are non-empty so config changes trigger the correct image.

[REDACTED]

input.name: Runner image packages
input.overview: Split core apt packages into a maintainable manifest file consumed by the Dockerfile; add a **global** YAML list of extra apt packages (your choice) that is written into the remote build context and baked at image build time, with an image tag suffix so Docker does not reuse the wrong image when extras change.
input.plan:
# Container runner image: core manifest + global extra packages

## Context (current behavior)

- The image is defined in [`internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/Dockerfile`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/Dockerfile) and embedded via [`internal/runner/assets.go`](internal/runner/assets.go).
- [`buildAgenticRunnerImage`](internal/runner/container.go) writes `Dockerfile`, `entrypoint.sh`, and `docker-wrapper.sh` to `/tmp/gh-sr-agentic-runner-build` on the **host over SSH**, then runs `docker build`.
- The tag is `gh-sr/agentic-runner:<actions-runner-version>` only ([`setupContainer`](internal/runner/container.go) ~L77). All `runner_mode: container` runners on a host share that tag today.

Because you chose **global** extras (one list for the whole config), there is a single “variant” of the image per host per `gh sr` invocation—no per-runner tag matrix. You still **must** change the tag when the global extra list changes, otherwise `containerImageExists` will skip the build and containers keep the old layers.

## Design principles

1. **Declarative core list** — one package per line, comments allowed; same file used at build time via `COPY` + `xargs`/`apt-get install` (industry-standard pattern for “Dockerfile stays thin, lists are diff-friendly”).
2. **Declarative global extras** — YAML on `Config`, validated (allowlist package names) to avoid shell/heredoc injection when writing remote files.
3. **Deterministic image identity** — append a short stable suffix to the tag when `extra_apt_packages` is non-empty, e.g. `gh-sr/agentic-runner:2.320.0-xa1b2c3d` derived from sorted package names (and optionally a hash of the embedded core manifest so a gh-sr upgrade that only changes core packages still invalidates the tag; see “Optional refinement” below).
4. **Layer ordering** — keep heavy/stable steps (Docker CE, `gh`, runner tarball, gh-aw) unchanged; run **core** `apt-get` early from the manifest; run **extras** in a **later** layer after `COPY apt-packages-extra.txt` so editing extras does not invalidate earlier layers more than necessary.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph repo [Repo / embed]
    Dockerfile[Dockerfile]
    CoreTxt[apt-packages-core.txt]
    Scripts[entrypoint + docker-wrapper]
  end
  subgraph local [gh sr CLI]
    YAML[runners.yml Config]
    BuildCtx[Write build context over SSH]
  end
  subgraph host [Runner host]
    DockerBuild[docker build]
    Image[Tagged image]
  end
  CoreTxt --> Dockerfile
  YAML --> BuildCtx
  Dockerfile --> BuildCtx
  Scripts --> BuildCtx
  BuildCtx --> DockerBuild
  DockerBuild --> Image
```

## 1. Core system dependencies → separate file

- Add [`internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt): one Debian package name per line, `#` comments, blank lines ignored.
- Move the current `apt-get install` list from the Dockerfile into this file (preserve order or sort for consistency—sorted is easier to maintain and dedupe).
- Dockerfile replaces the long inline list with something equivalent to:
  - `COPY apt-packages-core.txt /tmp/apt-packages-core.txt`
  - `RUN apt-get update && xargs -r -a /tmp/apt-packages-core.txt apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*`
- Embed the new file in Go ([`internal/runner/assets.go`](internal/runner/assets.go) `//go:embed`) and extend `buildAgenticRunnerImage` to write it alongside the Dockerfile (same heredoc / delimiter-escaping pattern as today).
- **Small cleanup**: drop duplicate `libvips` entry in the current Dockerfile list when migrating to the file.

Keep Docker CE / `gh` / runner / gh-aw blocks as separate `RUN`s (different apt sources and keys); only the “Ubuntu main” core set moves into the manifest.

## 2. Global config for extra tools

- Extend [`internal/config/config.go`](internal/config/config.go) `Config` with an optional block, e.g.:

  ```yaml
  container_runner_image:
    extra_apt_packages:
      - ffmpeg
      - sqlite3
  ```

  (Exact field names can be bikeshedded; prefer a nested struct over a flat top-level list for future fields like `extra_apt_repos` if you ever need PPAs.)

- Validation in existing config validation path: non-empty names, allowlist `[a-z0-9.+-]+` (Debian package token), max count / max line length to avoid abuse.
- Document in [`internal/config/runners.yml.template`](internal/config/runners.yml.template) and a short note in [`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md) (container section).

## 3. Build pipeline changes

- **Tag computation** (in [`internal/runner/container.go`](internal/runner/container.go)): centralize a helper, e.g. `containerRunnerImageTag(actionsRunnerVersion, extraPackages []string) string`. If `len(extraPackages)==0`, keep today’s tag. If non-empty, append `-x` + first 8–12 hex chars of `SHA256(join(sorted(names), "\n"))` (stable across hosts).
- **Pass extras into build**: `ops.Setup` / `RebuildImage` already receive `*config.Config` in [`internal/ops/ops.go`](internal/ops/ops.go); thread `cfg.ContainerRunnerImage.ExtraAptPackages` into `Manager` (either a new optional field on `Manager` set before runner loops, or add a parameter to `Setup` / `RebuildImage` / `buildAgenticRunnerImage`—pick one style and use it consistently).
- **`buildAgenticRunnerImage`**: write `apt-packages-extra.txt` (empty file or one package per line). Dockerfile `COPY`s it and runs `RUN` only if the file has content (`grep -q .` / `test -s`) to avoid pointless `apt-get` when empty—or always run `xargs -r` (empty file = no-op).
- **Tests**: extend [`internal/runner/container_test.go`](internal/runner/container_test.go) tag/build expectations; add config validation tests for package names.

## 4. Optional refinement (recommended if you want fewer “wrong image” surprises)

- Include a **hash of the embedded `apt-packages-core.txt` content** in the tag suffix **always** (or whenever any container runner exists), so upgrading `gh sr` with a changed core manifest automatically uses a new tag without relying on users to `rebuild`. Tradeoff: more image rebuilds after upgrades even when extras are empty.

## 5. Out of scope (v1)

- **Per-runner** package lists (you ruled out for now).
- **Extra apt repositories / PPAs** or arbitrary shell snippets (high security and caching complexity); v1 stays Ubuntu + existing Docker/`gh` sources + main `extra_apt_packages`.

## Implementation touchpoints (concise)

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| New file | `apt-packages-core.txt` + embed + remote write |
| Dockerfile | `COPY` manifests; split core vs extra install `RUN`s |
| Config | New struct + YAML + validation |
| `container.go` | Tag helper; `buildAgenticRunnerImage` signature + extra file write; `setupContainer` / `rebuildContainerImage` use new tag |
| `ops.go` | Pass global extras from `cfg` into `Manager` or build calls |
| Docs / template | Example + “change list → image tag changes / or run `gh sr rebuild`” |

todos.content: Add apt-packages-core.txt, update Dockerfile to COPY+xargs install, go:embed + write in buildAgenticRunnerImage
Tag suffix when extras non-empty; thread cfg extras through ops→Manager→buildAgenticRunnerImage; write apt-packages-extra.txt
Update container_test tag/build expectations; config tests for package name validation

input.new_string:
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Core system dependencies (manifest: apt-packages-core.txt)
COPY apt-packages-core.txt /tmp/apt-packages-core.txt
RUN apt-get update \
    && grep -v '^[[:space:]]*#' /tmp/apt-packages-core.txt \
        | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*$' \
        | xargs -r apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

input.old_string:
# gh-aw CLI extension
RUN su - runner -c "curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/gh-aw/main/install-gh-aw.sh | bash"

# Runner state directory (bind-mounted from host at runtime)
input.new_string:
# gh-aw CLI extension
RUN su - runner -c "curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/gh-aw/main/install-gh-aw.sh | bash"

# Optional extra apt packages (from runners.yml; empty file skips this step)
COPY apt-packages-extra.txt /tmp/apt-packages-extra.txt
RUN apt-get update \
    && if [ -s /tmp/apt-packages-extra.txt ]; then \
         grep -v '^[[:space:]]*#' /tmp/apt-packages-extra.txt \
             | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*$' \
             | xargs -r apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends; \
       fi \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Runner state directory (bind-mounted from host at runtime)

If you set global `container_runner_image.extra_apt_packages` in `runners.yml`, the tag becomes `gh-sr/agentic-runner:<version>-x<8-hex>` so Docker does not reuse an image built without those packages.

input.old_string: gh-sr will build the runner container image locally on first `gh sr setup` (Ubuntu 24.04 + Docker CE + gh-aw + dnsmasq + actions runner). Subsequent setups skip the build if the image is already up to date.
input.new_string: gh-sr will build the runner container image locally on first `gh sr setup` (Ubuntu 24.04 + Docker CE + gh-aw + dnsmasq + actions runner). Core apt dependencies live in the repo manifest `internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt` (baked into the gh-sr binary). Optional tools: add a global `container_runner_image.extra_apt_packages` list in `runners.yml` (Debian package names only). Subsequent setups skip the build if the image for that tag already exists; after changing the extra list or upgrading gh-sr when the core manifest changed, run `gh sr rebuild` for container-mode runners if needed.

input.new_string:
Long: `Rebuilds the gh-sr/agentic-runner Docker image from the embedded sources
(same image for all runner_mode: container runners, agentic or not), recreates
the runner containers, and starts them. The image includes global
container_runner_image.extra_apt_packages from runners.yml when set (see the
agentic workflows guide).

content.text:
Here is what was implemented.

### Core manifest
- Added [`internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt): sorted Ubuntu packages (duplicate `libvips` removed).
- [`internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/Dockerfile`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/Dockerfile): `COPY` + `grep` + `xargs -r apt-get install` for core; later `COPY apt-packages-extra.txt` + conditional install after gh-aw.
- [`internal/runner/assets.go`](internal/runner/assets.go): `go:embed` for the core manifest.
- [`buildAgenticRunnerImage`](internal/runner/container.go): writes `apt-packages-core.txt` and `apt-packages-extra.txt` on the build host (`: >` when extras are empty).

### Global config
- [`internal/config/config.go`](internal/config/config.go): `container_runner_image.extra_apt_packages` on `Config`, validation (Debian-style names, max 256 entries, max name length 200), and `ContainerRunnerImageExtraAptPackages()` returning a copy.
- [`internal/ops/ops.go`](internal/ops/ops.go): `applyContainerImageExtras` before **Setup**, **Up**, **RebuildImage**, and **Update** so `Manager.ContainerImageExtraApt` matches the loaded config.

### Image tag
- [`ContainerRunnerImageTag`](internal/runner/container.go): `gh-sr/agentic-runner:<version>` unchanged with no extras; with extras, `-x` + first 8 hex chars of `SHA256(sorted unique names joined by \n)`.
- [`setupContainer`](internal/runner/container.go) / [`rebuildContainerImage`](internal/runner/container.go) use that tag and pass extras into `buildAgenticRunnerImage`.

### Docs / CLI
- [`internal/config/runners.yml.template`](internal/config/runners.yml.template) and [`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md) updated.
- [`cmd/gh-sr/main.go`](cmd/gh-sr/main.go): `gh sr rebuild` long help mentions `container_runner_image`.

### Tests
- [`internal/config/config_test.go`](internal/config/config_test.go): validation, too-many-packages, copy behavior.
- [`internal/runner/container_test.go`](internal/runner/container_test.go): `TestContainerRunnerImageTag` (including stable `-x908d9db2` for `ffmpeg` + `sqlite3`).

`go test ./...` and `go build ./cmd/gh-sr` succeed. All related todos are completed.

[REDACTED]
